title: Chasing Autonomy: Dynamic Retargeting and Control Guided RL for Performant and Controllable Humanoid Running
abstract: Humanoid robots have the promise of locomoting like humans, including fast and dynamic running. Recently, reinforcement learning (RL) controllers that can mimic human motions have become popular as they can generate very dynamic behaviors, but they are often restricted to single motion play-back which hinders their deployment in long duration and autonomous locomotion. In this paper, we present a pipeline to dynamically retarget human motions through an optimization routine with hard constraints to generate improved periodic reference libraries from a single human demonstration. We then study the effect of both the reference motion and the reward structure on the reference and commanded velocity tracking, concluding that a goal-conditioned and control-guided reward which tracks dynamically optimized human data results in the best performance. We deploy the policy on hardware, demonstrating its speed and endurance by achieving running speeds of up to 3.3 m/s on a Unitree G1 robot and traversing hundreds of meters in real-world environments. Additionally, to demonstrate the controllability of the locomotion, we use the controller in a full perception and planning autonomy stack for obstacle avoidance while running outdoors.
summary:
# 论文总结：Chasing Autonomy: Dynamic Retargeting and Control Guided RL for Performant and Controllable Humanoid Running

## 1. 核心创新点

Chasing Autonomy 的关键不是让 humanoid 复播放一段人类跑步动作，而是从单个人类 demonstration 动态 retarget 出更好的周期参考库，再用 目标条件化、control-guided RL 奖励训练可控长距离 running policy。它把“像人一样跑”和“能自主持续跑”两个目标连起来。

## 2. 技术创新拆解

- **动态重定向参考库**：用带硬约束的优化 routine 从单个人类 demonstration 生成改进的 periodic reference libraries，避免 policy 只能跟踪一段固定 motion playback。
- **控制引导的目标条件奖励**：比较 参考动作 与 reward structure 对速度跟踪和命令跟踪的影响，最终用 目标条件化/control-guided reward 让跑步既快又可控。
- **户外自主栈集成**：公开摘要确认策略接入 perception and planning autonomy stack，用于真实户外跑步避障，而不是只在跑步机或短距离直线演示。

## 3. 方法论突破

- **新颖性**：与常见 motion imitation controller 的单片段播放不同，该工作把 reference library 优化、速度命令跟踪和自主避障放在同一 running pipeline 中。
- **关键技术**：核心手段是 constrained dynamic retargeting、periodic reference library、目标条件化 policy、control-guided reward，以及 Unitree G1 硬件部署。
- **理论支撑**：公开摘要没有强调新理论；主要贡献在动态参考生成和硬件系统验证。

## 4. 实验验证

- **主要基准/场景**：真实硬件平台是 Unitree G1；验证包括高速跑、长距离真实环境穿越，以及接入感知规划栈的户外避障。
- **性能提升**：公开摘要报告最高 running speed 达到 3.3 m/s，并能在真实环境中 traversing hundreds of meters。
- **消融实验**：作者研究了 参考动作 和 reward structure 对 reference/commanded velocity tracking 的影响，结论是动态优化人类数据加 目标条件化/control-guided reward 表现最好。

## 5. 局限与启发

- **当前局限**：方法仍依赖高质量人类 demonstration、retargeting 优化和 Unitree G1 的动力学条件；复杂地形、强接触扰动或人群环境中的安全性还需要更多实测。
- **未来方向**：后续可扩展到多速度 gait library、崎岖地形、在线 reference adaptation 和更强感知规划闭环。
- **可迁移性**：对 humanoid running、快速巡检和户外移动很有参考价值；迁移到其他 humanoid 时，reference dynamics、奖励权重和安全约束需要重调。

## 6. 一句话总结

这篇论文最值得记住的是：它让 Unitree G1 不只是模仿跑步，而是能以 3.3 m/s 级速度长距离、可控地户外自主跑。

recognized_fields.title: Chasing Autonomy: Dynamic Retargeting and Control Guided RL for Performant and Controllable Humanoid Running
recognized_fields.abstract: Humanoid robots have the promise of locomoting like humans, including fast and dynamic running. Recently, reinforcement learning (RL) controllers that can mimic human motions have become popular as they can generate very dynamic behaviors, but they are often restricted to single motion play-back which hinders their deployment in long duration and autonomous locomotion. In this paper, we present a pipeline to dynamically retarget human motions through an optimization routine with hard constraints to generate improved periodic reference libraries from a single human demonstration. We then study the effect of both the reference motion and the reward structure on the reference and commanded velocity tracking, concluding that a goal-conditioned and control-guided reward which tracks dynamically optimized human data results in the best performance. We deploy the policy on hardware, demonstrating its speed and endurance by achieving running speeds of up to 3.3 m/s on a Unitree G1 robot and traversing hundreds of meters in real-world environments. Additionally, to demonstrate the controllability of the locomotion, we use the controller in a full perception and planning autonomy stack for obstacle avoidance while running outdoors.
